puzzle0.title: RELAY ACCESS
puzzle0.description: Access code required. Use the active relay authorization, not the deprecated fragment.

puzzle1.title: RELAY POWER ROUTING
puzzle1.description: Match unstable power nodes with the correct station systems.

event1.text: Can you hear me?

event2.text: Please don't be another automated relay echo. I have been losing arguments with machines for weeks.

event6.text: I'm Dr. Ethan Hale. Chief Engineer, Orbital Research Station ECHO.

event7.text: And before you ask, no, my official emergency plan was not supposed to be begging a stranger through a half-dead relay.

event1.text: I kept the relay spine alive after the blackout. Which sounds heroic until you remember the relay is currently coughing blood.
No. That's fair. Annoying, but fair.

event2.text: Oxygen is at thirty-two percent. Suit battery says forty, which means twenty if it's feeling honest.

event4.title: BIOMETRIC FRAGMENT: HALE
event4.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
HEART RATE: ELEVATED
OXYGEN: 32%
SUIT LINK: UNSTABLE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 81%
event4.caption: Partial telemetry fragment transmitted through the relay.

event0.text: Something happened here. Something very wrong.

event1.text: The station went dark. Comms folded. Internal routing broke into pieces.

event2.text: You're the first signal I've had in months that didn't sound like a ghost wearing someone else's mouth.

event0.text: Good. I needed to hear that more than I want to admit.

event1.text: But don't confuse helping me with trusting me. Those are different wires. Cross them and something sparks.
Ask questions. Just don't take forever. The station has a habit of punishing hesitation.

event0.text: I'm sending the only crew record I can still access.

event1.text: If it cuts out halfway, blame the relay. Or the corpse of the relay. It was never very good at dying gracefully.

event2.title: CREW LOG: ETHAN HALE
event2.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE — CHIEF ENGINEER
RELAY MAINTENANCE AUTHORIZATION: 73_1
STATUS: ACTIVE

NOTE: Secondary maintenance index lists deprecated fragment: _319.
Current authorization must be reconstructed from active record.
event2.caption: Recovered identity and maintenance fragment. Authorization appears partially damaged.

event3.text: That code should still open the relay door, but the file is damaged.

event4.text: You'll need to read it properly. Current authorization, not deprecated fragment.

choice0.text: CAN YOU JUST TELL ME THE CODE?

event0.text: I would love to. Truly. Unfortunately, my memory currently has the structural integrity of wet paper.

event1.text: The log says active authorization starts with seventy-three and ends with one. The old fragment has a nine in it.

event0.text: The relay room should still be operational.

event1.text: Should.
Wrong code. Don't panic. Panic is how machines convince humans to make a second worse decision.

event2.text: The panel noticed. Of course it noticed. It has one job and apparently it's good at being offended.

event3.text: Try again. Active relay authorization. Not the deprecated fragment.

event1.text: Good. Either you're very smart, or I accidentally taught a stranger how to break into my station.

event2.text: Let's pretend it's the first one.

event4.text: Door's open, but the relay spine is starving. I need you to route power before this link collapses again.

event1.text: No, no— don't feed the relay from reserve. Life support gets reserve. Relay gets the unstable node because apparently everything today is a moral compromise.

event2.text: Reset the board. Slowly. If the relay screams, let it scream. Life support gets first rights to anything clean.

choice1.text: ASK HALE TO EXPLAIN THE ROUTING.

event3.text: Still terrible. But now it's professionally terrible.

event0.text: I was hoping you didn't notice that.

event1.text: Which is a stupid hope, since you are apparently the only competent thing connected to this relay.

event2.text: Something is listening through the carrier. Not a person. Not exactly.

event0.text: Good. One nightmare at a time.

event1.text: I can work with one nightmare. I have a system.
Did you hear that?

event5.text: No. Sorry. That's a stupid thing to say.

event6.text: Don't ignore it. Just don't obey it without thinking.

event1.text: You heard a voice from nowhere and immediately handed it the steering wheel.

event2.text: Fine. If it wants Door 12, we check Door 12. But we do it with records, not obedience.

event1.text: That is the closest thing to a survival philosophy I have left.

event1.title: SECURITY LOG
event1.content:
21:17 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Manual override accepted.
21:18 — Operator identity redacted.
21:20 — Internal sensor flagged decompression risk.
event1.caption: Recovered security event from the station archive.

event3.title: MEDICAL REPORT
event3.content:
21:20 — Casualty reported.
Cause: decompression trauma.
Location: Door 12 access corridor.
Status: fatal.
event3.caption: Recovered emergency medical report.

event4.title: CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT
event4.content:
SOURCE: DOOR 12 SECURITY FEED
FRAME STATUS: VISUAL DATA CORRUPTED
ANALYSIS RESULT:
- One subject detected outside Door 12.
- Subject biometric outline matches Ethan Hale.
- Object detected in subject's right hand: manual override handle.
- Frame terminated before decompression event.
event4.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted visual feed. No image recovered.
event4.message: [CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

choice1.text: DO YOU REMEMBER ANY OF THIS?

event0.text: I can't explain evidence I don't remember making.

event1.text: And every time you say my name like a charge being read in court, it gets harder to think.
A warning alarm. Door 12. Someone shouting my name like I had already done something unforgivable.

backgroundEvent0.text: ASK HIM WHAT HAPPENED AFTER HE OPENED THE DOOR

choice1.text: KEEP THE CHANNEL OPEN A LITTLE LONGER.

event0.text: Look at the damaged authorization like a bad sentence, not a locked door.

event1.text: Active record: seventy-three, blank, one. Deprecated fragment: three-one-nine. The system warns you not to use the whole old fragment. It doesn't say every piece of it is useless.

event2.text: So we borrow the only digit that fits and hope the relay is less vindictive than it looks.

event0.text: Archive cache can survive on the stable low draw. It doesn't need heroics, just patience.

event1.text: Life support gets emergency reserve because I am annoyingly attached to breathing.

event2.text: The relay spine takes the unstable high draw. Dangerous, ugly, but enough to push our signal through.

event1.text: Good. Put the evidence somewhere the station can't chew on it.

event2.text: And Operator? If Door 12 comes up again, don't let me dodge it. I don't trust my memory enough to protect you from it.

event1.text: DO NOT LET HIM CLOSE THE RECORD

event2.text: You kept the channel open. Brave, reckless, or both. Usually those die in the same corridor.

event4.text: ASK HIM WHO WAS OUTSIDE DOOR 12
